topic: A person finds out their article was published under someone else's name
raw_output:
[story 1]

The email landed in my inbox, and for a full minute, I just stared at the screen, my breath catching in my throat. Then, a slow, unstoppable smile spread across my face, starting at my eyes and pulling at the corners of my mouth until it felt stretched to its limit. I reread the attribution line, my fingers trembling slightly as I confirmed the name—*not* mine. A sharp, exhilarating rush flooded my chest, making me want to laugh out loud, a sound that felt too big for the quiet office. I immediately started typing a reply, my hands flying across the keyboard with an almost frantic energy, already planning how to leverage this unexpected windfall. The sheer audacity of it made my whole body hum with vibrant energy.

[story 2]

When the notification popped up, I physically leaned back in my chair, letting out a slow, shaky exhale that seemed to release all the tension I hadn't realized I was holding. My heart began to pound a steady, joyful rhythm against my ribs. I clicked on the link, and there it was: my piece, perfectly polished, under a completely different moniker. A wide grin broke through my composure, wide enough to show all my teeth. I stood up abruptly, pacing the small space between my desk and the window, running a hand through my hair. "This is incredible," I murmured to the empty room, the words sounding breathless and utterly sincere. The world suddenly felt brighter, saturated with a dizzying sense of triumph.

[story 3]

I reread the publication details three times, my eyes scanning the text until the words swam into focus. A sudden, buoyant lightness lifted my shoulders. A genuine, unrestrained laugh bubbled up, startling a nearby colleague. It wasn't a polite chuckle; it was a deep, full sound that resonated in my chest. I felt a powerful urge to jump up and down, a restless energy demanding release. I picked up my phone, my thumb hovering over the call button, a bright, almost manic excitement bubbling just beneath my skin. This wasn't just good news; this was everything I’d been hoping for, and the sheer weight of it felt wonderfully, thrillingly light.
